title: hydrangea × versicolor
intro: Hydrangea × versicolor, also known as Panicle Hydrangea, is a popular ornamental shrub native to Asia and North America, characterized by its large, cone-shaped flowers and broad, deciduous leaves. In traditional medicine, the roots and stems of Hydrangea × versicolor are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant. Hydrangea × versicolor is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried roots and stems being steeped in hot water to release their medicinal compounds.
